Empires & Puzzles Vanda 5 Star Legendary Fire Red from Halloween Family Review

A deep dive into Vanda, the Halloween hero who introduced Very Fast immunity to the game. Does her base form still hold up against the creeping shadows of power, or is she just a shadow of her former self?
Vanda: The Original Vampire Queen of Speed
Vanda, The Crimson Speeder: Ailment Immunity in a Flash

Vanda is a Legendary Fire hero from the Morlovia (Halloween) family, originally released in 2020. As a Sorcerer class hero operating at Very Fast speed, she was designed to be a rapid-response support unit, capable of shielding her team from status ailments before the enemy could blink. While her costume has since stolen the spotlight (and the fashion awards), the base version of Vanda remains a distinct entity with slightly different mechanics. This review focuses on the classic "Vampire Queen" form. And yes, those bats holding her dress are still the hardest working minions in the game.

Vanda's design is classic gothic horror meets high fashion. She sports a dark, elegant crimson gown supported by literal bats, which is both practical for a vampire and terrible for dry cleaning. Her expression is one of confident menace, fitting for a hero who plans to steal your healing and laugh about it. Compared to her mafia-themed costume, the base art feels more "traditional vampire royalty," leaning into the supernatural roots of the Morlovia family.
Vanda's base stats reflect her age.

When looking at her stats in the context of the 2026 meta, Vanda shows her age significantly. Her base power of 795 is well below the modern standard of 1250+. Her attack (774) and defense (735) are fragile by today's standards, where tanky heroes easily exceed 1300 defense. She relies heavily on emblems, limit breaks, and her costume bonus to survive against modern snipers who can easily one-shot her old stat line.
The core of Vanda's kit is the "Twilight of Sorrow."
125% to all enemies is negligible damage in the current era. It serves more to strip layers from fighters or poke enemies than to secure kills.
Stealing 61% of healing is a unique mechanic. It turns enemy healers against their own team. If a Rigard tries to heal, Vanda takes the lion's share.
The crown jewel is the status ailment immunity. However, on the base version, this only lasts for

The base version deals slightly more damage (125% vs 115%) and steals more healing (61% vs 51%) than her costume. However, the costume extends the immunity duration to 3 turns. In a game where timing is everything, that extra turn is often worth the trade-off in raw numbers, making the base version's 2-turn window feel very tight and unforgiving.
Very Fast (6.5 tiles). With a level 11 Mana Troop (or even lower with costume bonus/emblems), she charges in 6 tiles. This is her biggest asset.
~774 Attack x 125% = ~967 damage (raw). Against modern defense (1300+), this will likely result in double-digit damage or low triple digits per hero. It's a "soft" AOE.
If an enemy healer would heal 1000 HP, Vanda steals 610 HP, and the enemy only receives 390. This effectively acts as a 610 HP self-heal.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Morlovia family has received updates to stay relevant.

The updated family bonus provides significant stats (Attack/Defense/Health/Mana Gen) when paired with other Halloween heroes. The Mana Generation bonus is particularly juicy for a Very Fast hero, making her even easier to break tile breakpoints.

This is a fantastic modern addition. A 65% chance to steal the newest buff (or deal extra damage if none exist) adds utility. Stealing a taunt, dodge, or defense up at Very Fast speed can disrupt the enemy's opening significantly.
This fits her theme perfectly. Healing herself when dealing damage (or attacking normal enemies) helps her sustain, which is crucial given her outdated defense stats.
Upon release, Vanda was a direct answer to the Gravemaker/Telluria/Vela meta. Her ability to block the water damage and burn was invaluable. She was "Grazul with teeth."

Her stats have fallen behind, and the 2-turn immunity on the base skill feels too short compared to newer heroes (like her own costume or Garnet). The damage component has become irrelevant for kills.

Base Vanda is largely overshadowed by her costume. The 2-turn duration is simply too hard to time effectively in modern wars. However, if you lack the costume, she remains a niche counterpick in Rush Wars or against heavy ailment teams if you can time her perfectly. She is a

The difference between 2 and 3 turns of immunity is massive. It allows for a much larger margin of error and keeps your team safe for a significant portion of the match. The costume also updates her class to Wizard (superior for damage/dispelling buffs via Jinx) from Sorcerer.

Vanda - Black Knight - (Healer) - Marjana - (Sniper). Vanda protects the team early while Black Knight taunts.
Vanda + Cleansers. Use Vanda to proactively block. If she isn't ready, use a cleanser.
Since her immunity is a buff, any dispeller (Kageburado, Sabina) removes it easily.

Heroes that bypass buffs ignore her protection.
If you pull Vanda early, she is a game-changer. Her speed allows you to punch up against teams that rely on status ailments. Prioritize her survivability.

Base Vanda is a relic. If you have the costume, use it. If not, she is a very situational war depth hero for your 5th or 6th red team, specifically to counter Alfrike or heavy DoT teams. Do not prioritize Alpha Aethers on the base version unless you are a dedicated collector or have a specific niche strategy.
The community has always had a love-hate relationship with Vanda's base form.
"She put Grazul to shame. Fantastic pull."
- An early adopter praising her over the previous ailment blocker.
"Drat, now I noticed that costume just adds one round of protection... Me sad."
- A player realizing the nuance of the costume release.
"This is the one I always wanted from the portal and she still eludes me."
- She remains a coveted hero for her unique mechanics.

Vanda is the classic "fast and furious" support hero. In her base form, she lives life in the fast lane, providing protection that is potent but fleeting. She is a reminder of a time when 2 turns of immunity felt like an eternity. Today, she serves as a solid, if slightly dusty, tool in the red arsenal. If you have her costume, wear it. If not, treat her like a sports car with a small gas tank—fun for a quick spin, but don't count on her for the long haul.
A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Vanda, the Very Fast Legendary Fire hero from the Halloween event. We analyze her stats, skills, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
